title: acaena suaveolens
intro: Acaena suaveolens, also known as the woolly cat's ear, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Acaena suaveolens include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
